articles.title: QRVEY, AND RESOLVER
articles.summary: What does the latest research say about the adoption of GenAI in SaaS products? Watch this RECORDED PANEL DISCUSSION [4] for expert insights and actionable advice from AI analysts and tech product leaders as they cover:

articles.title: WATCH NOW OR READ THE TRANSCRIPT (UNGATED)
ROI: SEDUCTIVE BUT OF LIMITED VALUE IN PRODUCT PLANNING
articles.summary: ROI, a fundamental tool for financial decisions, is unreliable for product and engineering planning due to uncertainty in both development costs and future revenue projections. Instead of relying on misleading ROI calculations, product leaders should focus on strategic alignment, broad revenue ranges, realistic team costs, and reframing discussions to highlight trade-offs and risks, ensuring that investment decisions are grounded in market insight rather than false precision.

articles.title: ANOTHER ONE
articles.summary: The rise of open-source LLMs like DeepSeek has shattered assumptions about AI development, showing that state-of-the-art models can emerge rapidly, disrupting markets and making proprietary models less defensible. This shift suggests that the future of AI is not just about incremental improvements but about a world where thousands of unpredictable models emerge, reshaping industries in ways that go far beyond automation and efficiency.

articles.title: CUSTOMERS DON'T CARE ABOUT YOUR AI FEATURE
articles.summary: Overusing "AI-powered" in marketing can lower expectations and fail to build trust or justify higher prices. Instead, focus on clear, tangible benefits and real outcomes that solve user problems to drive engagement and loyalty.

articles.title: LONE WOLF OR OVER-COLLABORATOR?
articles.summary: Product managers risk imbalance: Lone Wolves work in isolation, facing missed context and pushback, while Over-Collaborators over-prioritize consensus, leading to delays and inefficiency. Lone Wolves should seek feedback and communicate updates, while Over-Collaborators should limit meetings and focus on deadlines. Balancing collaboration and focus is crucial for success.

articles.title: ALLOCATION MODELS: THE C-SUITE IMPERATIVE
articles.summary: Allocation models help prioritize and focus resources effectively, preventing scattered efforts and misalignment. Teams can align and make consistent trade-offs by clearly defining priorities. Regular communication, conflict resolution, and reinforcement of these models ensure teams stay focused, driving greater impact and preventing resource dilution.

articles.title: WHY ULTRA-SUCCESSFUL PRODUCTS DISAPPEAR DAILY
articles.summary: Viral products like Wordle, BeReal, and Thursday thrive by limiting availability and boosting retention and social buzz through scarcity and shared experiences. Strategies like daily exclusives, power hours, or time-bound events turn products into must-use moments, increasing engagement and word of mouth. Less access drives more excitement and virality.

articles.title: THE AI INFRASTRUCTURE BUILD-OUT DEBATE JUST GOT DEEPER
articles.summary: DeepSeek's R1 release has disrupted AI infrastructure debates by proving that high-performing models can be trained at significantly lower costs, challenging the massive capital expenditures of Big Tech. While this raises questions about AI infrastructure overspending, some argue that efficiency gains could actually increase compute demand, signaling a shift in focus from sheer growth to margin optimization and inference scaling.

articles.title: WHY DEEPSEEK IS A GIFT TO THE AMERICAN PEOPLE
articles.summary: DeepSeek's open-source leap reveals the failure of restrictive U.S. AI policies, accelerating AI's Jevons effect - greater efficiency driving greater demand. To stay competitive, the U.S. must embrace open innovation, reduce regulation, and promote entrepreneurship to lead the AI revolution.

articles.title: OPENAI IS AN APP COMPANY NOW
articles.summary: OpenAI must sustain product innovation and user engagement to stay ahead of growing competition like DeepSeek.

articles.title: IN 2025, STOP TRYING TO GET BETTER AT OKRS
articles.summary: Teams should focus on treating OKRs as a product that measures progress toward strategic priorities rather than striving for technical correctness.
